ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io API (swsh5-72). National Pokedex no. 472. Artist: ryoma uratsuka. Bat-scorpion silhouette is unambiguous Gliscor. Pokemon Company internal IP, no external crossover risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Uncommon from Sword & Shield Battle Styles (swsh5), released March 19, 2021. Stage 1 Fighting-type evolving from Gligar. Artist: ryoma uratsuka. Regulation mark E.
National Pokedex no. 472. Ground/Flying dual-type introduced Generation IV (Sinnoh). Evolves from Gligar when leveled up holding a Razor Fang at night — nocturnal item-based evolution mechanic.
sources: Serebii Pokedex no. 472, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: "Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey… Then its elongated fangs do the rest." Soundless aerial ambush predator; barbed tail grabs prey, fangs finish. Card's Guillotine attack (90 damage) directly mirrors the fang-strike finishing move.

Gliscor's defining competitive trait: Poison Heal + Toxic Orb converts self-poison into passive healing and grants status immunity. Smogon OU placement across multiple generations as Spikes setter and Substitute-based stall-breaker.
sources: Smogon: Gliscor OU; Smogon Forums ORAS OU thread, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Despite nearly doubling in size during evolution (Gligar 1.1m to Gliscor 2.0m), Gliscor is lighter than its pre-evolution — consistent with vampire bat anatomy (hollow bones for flight).
sources: Serebii Pokedex no. 472; Bulbapedia: Gliscor, via WebSearch snippet

related_cards.name: Gligar
related_cards.note: Pre-evolution; Ground/Flying type from Gen II Johto; Gliscor's evolutionary predecessor

related_cards.name: Gliscor
related_cards.note: Reprinted across Gen IV era sets; Battle Styles is the Sword & Shield E-mark version

related_cards.name: Garchomp
related_cards.note: Fellow Sinnoh apex predator; Ground-type niche overlap in flavor and competitive framing
